source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his power shall be strengthened, but not by his own force: and he shall lay all things waste, and shall prosper, and do more than can be believed. And he shall destroy the mighty, and the people of the saints,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: According to his will, and craft shall be successful in his hand: and his heart shall be puffed up, and in the abundance of all things he shall kill many: and he shall rise up against the prince of princes, and shall be broken without hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the vision of the evening and the morning, which was told, is true: thou, therefore, seal up the vision, because it shall come to pass after many days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I, Daniel, languished, and was sick for some days: and when I was risen up, I did the king’s business, and I was astonished at the vision, and there was none that could interpret it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the first year of Darius, the son of Assuerus, of the seed of the Medes, who reigned over the kingdom of the Chaldeans:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first year of his reign I, Daniel, understood by books the number of the years, concerning which the word of the Lord came to Jeremias, the prophet, that seventy years should be accomplished of the desolation of Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I set my face to the Lord, my God, to pray and make supplication with fasting, and sackcloth, and ashes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I prayed to the Lord, my God, and I made my confession, and said: I beseech thee, O Lord God, great and terrible, who keepest the covenant, and mercy to them that love thee, and keep thy commandments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We have sinned, we have committed iniquity, we have done wickedly, and have revolted: and we have gone aside from thy commandments, and thy judgments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We have not hearkened to thy servants, the prophets, that have spoken in thy name to our kings, to our princes, to our fathers, and to all the people of the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To thee, O Lord, justice: but to us confusion of face, as at this day to the men of Juda, and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to all Israel, to them that are near, and to them that are far off, in all the countries whither thou hast driven them, for their iniquities, by which they have sinned against thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, to us belongeth confusion of face, to our princes, and to our fathers, that have sinned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But to thee, the Lord our God, mercy and forgiveness, for we have departed from thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we have not hearkened to the voice of the Lord, our God, to walk in his law, which he set before us by his servants, the prophets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all Israel have transgressed thy law, and have turned away from hearing thy voice, and the malediction, and the curse, which is written in the book of Moses, the servant of God, is fallen upon us, because we have sinned against him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he hath confirmed his words which he spoke against us, and against our princes that judged us, that he would bring in upon us a great evil, such as never was under all the heaven, according to that which hath been done in Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As it is written in the law of Moses, all this evil is come upon us: and we entreated not thy face, O Lord our God, that we might turn from our iniquities, and think on thy truth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord hath watched upon the evil, and hath brought it upon us: the Lord, our God, is just in all his works which he hath done: for we have not hearkened to his voice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now, O Lord, our God, who hast brought forth thy people out of the land of Egypt, with a strong hand, and hast made thee a name as at this day: we have sinned, we have committed iniquity,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, against all thy justice: let thy wrath and thy indignation be turned away, I beseech thee, from thy city, Jerusalem, and from thy holy mountain. For by reason of our sins, and the iniquities of our fathers, Jerusalem, and thy people, are a reproach to all that are round about us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now, therefore, O our God, hear the supplication of thy servant, and his prayers: and shew thy face upon thy sanctuary, which is desolate, for thy own sake.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Incline, O my God, thy ear, and hear: open thy eyes, and see our desolation, and the city upon which thy name is called: for it is not for our justifications that we present our prayers before thy face, but for the multitude of thy tender mercies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, hear: O Lord, be appeased: hearken, and do: delay not, for thy own sake, O my God: because thy name is invocated upon thy city, and upon thy people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now while I was yet speaking, and praying, and confessing my sins, and the sins of my people of Israel, and presenting my supplications in the sight of my God, for the holy mountain of my God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As I was yet speaking in prayer, behold the man, Gabriel, whom I had seen in the vision at the beginning, flying swiftly, touched me at the time of the evening sacrifice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he instructed me, and spoke to me, and said: O Daniel, I am now come forth to teach thee, and that thou mightest understand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the beginning of thy prayers the word came forth: and I am come to shew it to thee, because thou art a man of desires: therefore, do thou mark the word, and understand the vision.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seventy weeks are shortened upon thy people, and upon thy holy city, that transgression may be finished, and sin may have an end, and iniquity may be abolished; and everlasting justice may be brought; and vision and prophecy may be fulfilled; and the saint of saints may be anointed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Know thou, therefore, and take notice: that from the going forth of the word, to build up Jerusalem again, unto Christ, the prince, there shall be seven weeks, and sixty-two weeks: and the street shall be built again, and the walls, in straitness of times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after sixty-two weeks Christ shall be slain: and the people that shall deny him shall not be his. And a people, with their leader, that shall come, shall destroy the city, and the sanctuary: and the end thereof shall be waste, and after the end of the war the appointed desolation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall confirm the covenant with many, in one week: and in the half of the week the victim and the sacrifice shall fail: and there shall be in the temple the abomination of desolation: and the desolation shall continue even to the consummation, and to the end.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the third year of Cyrus, king of the Persians, a word was revealed to Daniel, surnamed Baltassar, and a true word, and great strength: and he understood the word: for there is need of understanding in a vision.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In those days I, Daniel, mourned the days of three weeks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I ate no desirable bread, and neither flesh, nor wine, entered into my mouth, neither was I anointed with ointment: till the days of three weeks were accomplished.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the four and twentieth day of the first month, I was by the great river, which is the Tigris.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I lifted up my eyes, and I saw: and behold a man clothed in linen, and his loins were girded with the finest gold:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his body was like the chrysolite, and his face as the appearance of lightning, and his eyes as a burning lamp: and his arms, and all downward even to the feet, like in appearance to glittering brass: and the voice of his word like the voice of a multitude.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I, Daniel alone, saw the vision: for the men that were with me saw it not: but an exceeding great terror fell upon them, and they fled away, and hid themselves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I, being left alone, saw this great vision: and there remained no strength in me, and the appearance of my countenance was changed in me, and I fainted away, and retained no strength.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I heard the voice of his words: and when I heard I lay in a consternation upon my face, and my face was close to the ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold a hand touched me, and lifted me up upon my knees, and upon the joints of my hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to me: Daniel, thou man of desires, understand the words that I speak to thee, and stand upright: for I am sent now to thee. And when he had said this word to me, I stood trembling.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to me: Fear not, Daniel: for from the first day that thou didst set thy heart to understand, to afflict thyself in the sight of thy God, thy words have been heard: and I am come for thy words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the prince of the kingdom of the Persians resisted me one and twenty days: and behold Michael, one of the chief princes, came to help me, and I remained there by the king of the Persians.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I am come to teach thee what things shall befall thy people in the latter days, for as yet the vision is for days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was speaking such words to me, I cast down my countenance to the ground, and held my peace.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold as it were the likeness of a son of man touched my lips: then I opened my mouth and spoke, and said to him that stood before me: O my lord, at the sight of thee my joints are loosed, and no strength hath remained in me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And how can the servant of my lord speak with my lord? for no strength remaineth in me; moreover, my breath is stopped.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore, he that looked like a man, touched me again, and strengthened me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Fear not, O man of desires, peace be to thee: take courage, and be strong. And when he spoke to me, I grew strong, and I said: Speak, O my lord, for thou hast strengthened me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Dost thou know wherefore I am come to thee? And now I will return, to fight against the prince of the Persians. When I went forth, there appeared the prince of the Greeks coming.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I will tell thee what is set down in the scripture of truth: and none is my helper in all these things, but Michael your prince.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from the first year of Darius, the Mede, I stood up, that he might be strengthened, and confirmed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now I will shew thee the truth. Behold, there shall stand yet three kings in Persia, and the fourth shall be enriched exceedingly above them all: and when he shall be grown mighty by his riches, he shall stir up all against the kingdom of Greece.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But there shall rise up a strong king, and shall rule with great power: and he shall do what he pleaseth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he shall come to his height, his kingdom shall be broken, and it shall be divided towards the four winds of the heaven: but not to his posterity, nor according to his power with which he ruled. For his kingdom shall be rent in pieces, even for strangers, besides these.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king of the south shall be strengthened, and one of his princes shall prevail over him, and he shall rule with great power: for his dominion shall be great.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after the end of years they shall be in league together: and the daughter of the king of the south shall come to the king of the north to make friendship, but she shall not obtain the strength of the arm, neither shall her seed stand: and she shall be given up, and her young men that brought her, and they that strengthened her in these times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a plant of the bud of her roots shall stand up: and he shall come with an army, and shall enter into the province of the king of the north: and he shall abuse them, and shall prevail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall also carry away captive into Egypt their gods, and their graven things, and their precious vessels of gold and silver: he shall prevail against the king of the north.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king of the south shall enter into the kingdom, and shall return to his own land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his sons shall be provoked, and they shall assemble a multitude of great forces: and he shall come with haste like a flood: and he shall return, and be stirred up, and he shall join battle with his force.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king of the south being provoked, shall go forth, and shall fight against the king of the north, and shall prepare an exceeding great multitude, and a multitude shall be given into his hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall take a multitude, and his heart shall be lifted up, and he shall cast down many thousands: but he shall not prevail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the king of the north shall return, and shall prepare a multitude much greater than before: and in the end of times, and years, he shall come in haste with a great army, and much riches.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in those times many shall rise up against the king of the south, and the children of prevaricators of thy people shall lift up themselves to fulfil the vision, and they shall fall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king of the north shall come, and shall cast up a mount, and shall take the best fenced cities: and the arms of the south shall not withstand, and his chosen ones shall rise up to resist, and they shall not have strength.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall come upon him, and do according to his pleasure, and there shall be none to stand against his face: and he shall stand in the glorious land, and it shall be consumed by his hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall set his face to come to possess all his kingdom, and he shall make upright conditions with him: and he shall give him a daughter of women, to overthrow it: and she shall not stand, neither shall she be for him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall turn his face to the islands, and shall take many: and he shall cause the prince of his reproach to cease, and his reproach shall be turned upon him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall turn his face to the empire of his own land, and he shall stumble, and fall, and shall not be found.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall stand up in his place one most vile, and unworthy of kingly honour: and in a few days he shall be destroyed, not in rage nor in battle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall stand up in his place one despised, and the kingly honour shall not be given him: and he shall come privately, and shall obtain the kingdom by fraud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the arms of the fighter shall be overcome before his face, and shall be broken: yea, also the prince of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after friendships, he will deal deceitfully with him: and he shall go up, and shall overcome with a small people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall enter into rich and plentiful cities: and he shall do that which his fathers never did, nor his fathers’ fathers: he shall scatter their spoils, and their prey, and their riches, and shall forecast devices against the best fenced places: and this until a time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his strength, and his heart, shall be stirred up against the king of the south, with a great army: and the king of the south shall be stirred up to battle with many and very strong succours: and they shall not stand, for they shall form designs against him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that eat bread with him, shall destroy him, and his army shall be overthrown: and many shall fall down slain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the heart of the two kings shall be to do evil, and they shall speak lies at one table, and they shall not prosper: because as yet the end is unto another time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall return into his land with much riches: and his heart shall be against the holy covenant, and he shall succeed, and shall return into his own land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the time appointed he shall return, and he shall come to the south, but the latter time shall not be like the former.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the galleys and the Romans shall come upon him, and he shall be struck, and shall return, and shall have indignation against the covenant of the sanctuary, and he shall succeed: and he shall return, and shall devise against them that have forsaken the covenant of the sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And arms shall stand on his part, and they shall defile the sanctuary of strength, and shall take away the continual sacrifice: and they shall place there the abomination unto desolation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And such as deal wickedly against the covenant shall deceitfully dissemble: but the people that know their God shall prevail and succeed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that are learned among the people shall teach many: and they shall fall by the sword, and by fire, and by captivity, and by spoil for many days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they shall have fallen, they shall be relieved with a small help: and many shall be joined to them deceitfully.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And some of the learned shall fall, that they may be tried, and may be chosen, and made white, even to the appointed time: because yet there shall be another time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king shall do according to his will, and he shall be lifted up, and shall magnify himself against every god: and he shall speak great things against the God of gods, and shall prosper, till the wrath be accomplished. For the determination is made.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall make no account of the God of his fathers: and he shall follow the lust of women, and he shall not regard any gods: for he shall rise up against all things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he shall worship the god Maozim, in his place: and a god whom his fathers knew not, he shall worship with gold, and silver, and precious stones, and things of great price.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall do this to fortify Maozim with a strange god, whom he hath acknowledged, and he shall increase glory, and shall give them power over many, and shall divide the land gratis.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And at the time prefixed the king of the south shall fight against him, and the king of the north shall come against him like a tempest, with chariots, and with horsemen, and with a great navy, and he shall enter into the countries, and shall destroy, and pass through.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall enter into the glorious land, and many shall fall: and these only shall be saved out of his hand, Edom, and Moab, and the principality of the children of Ammon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall lay his hand upon the lands: and the land of Egypt shall not escape.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall have power over the treasures of gold, and of silver, and all the precious things of Egypt: and he shall pass through Lybia, and Ethiopia.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And tidings out of the east, and out of the north, shall trouble him: and he shall come with a great multitude to destroy and slay many.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall fix his tabernacle, Apadno, between the seas, upon a glorious and holy mountain: and he shall come even to the top thereof, and none shall help him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But at that time shall Michael rise up, the great prince, who standeth for the children of thy people: and a time shall come, such as never was from the time that nations began, even until that time. And at that time shall thy people be saved, every one that shall be found written in the book.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And many of those that sleep in the dust of the earth, shall awake: some unto life everlasting, and others unto reproach, to see it always.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they that are learned, shall shine as the brightness of the firmament: and they that instruct many to justice, as stars for all eternity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou, O Daniel, shut up the words, and seal the book, even to the time appointed: many shall pass over, and knowledge shall be manifold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I, Daniel, looked, and behold as it were two others stood: one on this side upon the bank of the river, and another on that side, on the other bank of the river.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said to the man that was clothed in linen, that stood upon the waters of the river: How long shall it be to the end of these wonders?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I heard the man that was clothed in linen, that stood upon the waters of the river, when he had lifted up his right hand, and his left hand to heaven, and had sworn by him that liveth for ever, that it should be unto a time, and times, and half a time. And when the scattering of the band of the holy people shall be accomplished, all these things shall be finished.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I heard, and understood not. And I said: O my lord, what shall be after these things?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Go, Daniel, because the words are shut up, and sealed until the appointed time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Many shall be chosen, and made white, and shall be tried as fire: and the wicked shall deal wickedly, and none of the wicked shall understand, but the learned shall understand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from the time when the continual sacrifice shall be taken away, and the abomination unto desolation shall be set up, there shall be a thousand two hundred ninety days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed is he that waiteth, and cometh unto a thousand three hundred thirty-five days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But go thou thy ways until the time appointed: and thou shalt rest, and stand in thy lot unto the end of the days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now there was a man that dwelt in Babylon, and his name was Joakim:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he took a wife, whose name was Susanna, the daughter of Helcias, a very beautiful woman, and one that feared God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For her parents being just, had instructed their daughter according to the law of Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Joakim was very rich, and had an orchard near his house: and the Jews resorted to him, because he was the most honourable of them all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there were two of the ancients of the people appointed judges that year, of whom the Lord said: That iniquity came out from Babylon, from the ancient judges, that seemed to govern the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These men frequented the house of Joakim, and all that had any matters of judgment came to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the people departed away at noon, Susanna went in, and walked in her husband’s orchard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the old men saw her going in every day, and walking: and they were inflamed with lust towards her:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they perverted their own mind, and turned away their eyes, that they might not look unto heaven, nor remember just judgments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So they were both wounded with the love of her, yet they did not make known their grief one to the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they were ashamed to declare to one another their lust, being desirous to have to do with her:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they watched carefully every day to see her. And one said to the other:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let us now go home, for it is dinner time. So going out, they departed one from another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And turning back again, they came both to the same place: and asking one another the cause, they acknowledged their lust: and then they agreed together upon a time, when they might find her alone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it fell out, as they watched a fit day, she went in on a time, as yesterday and the day before, with two maids only, and was desirous to wash herself in the orchard: for it was hot weather.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there was nobody there, but the two old men that had hid themselves, and were beholding her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So she said to the maids: Bring me oil, and washing balls, and shut the doors of the orchard, that I may wash me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they did as she bade them: and they shut the doors of the orchard, and went out by a back door to fetch what she had commanded them, and they knew not that the elders were hid within.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now when the maids were gone forth, the two elders arose, and ran to her, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the doors of the orchard are shut, and nobody seeth us, and we are in love with thee: wherefore consent to us, and lie with us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thou wilt not, we will bear witness against thee, that a young man was with thee, and therefore thou didst send away thy maids from thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Susanna sighed, and said: I am straitened on every side: for if I do this thing, it is death to me: and if I do it not, I shall not escape your hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But it is better for me to fall into your hands without doing it, than to sin in the sight of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With that Susanna cried out with a loud voice: and the elders also cried out against her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And one of them ran to the door of the orchard, and opened it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So when the servants of the house heard the cry in the orchard, they rushed in by the back door, to see what was the matter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But after the old men had spoken, the servants were greatly ashamed: for never had there been any such word said of Susanna. And on the next day,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the people were come to Joakim, her husband, the two elders also came full of wicked device against Susanna, to put her to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they said before the people: Send to Susanna, daughter of Helcias, the wife of Joakim. And presently they sent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she came with her parents, and children and all her kindred.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Susanna was exceeding delicate, and beautiful to behold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But those wicked men commanded that her face should be uncovered, (for she was covered) that so at least they might be satisfied with her beauty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore her friends, and all her acquaintance wept.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the two elders rising up in the midst of the people, laid their hands upon her head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she weeping, looked up to heaven, for her heart had confidence in the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the elders said: As we walked in the orchard alone, this woman came in with two maids, and shut the doors of the orchard, and sent away the maids from her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then a young man that was there hid came to her, and lay with her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But we that were in a corner of the orchard, seeing this wickedness, ran up to them, and we saw them lie together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And him indeed we could not take, because he was stronger than us, and opening the doors, he leaped out:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But having taken this woman, we asked who the young man was, but she would not tell us: of this thing we are witnesses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The multitude believed them, as being the elders, and the judges of the people, and they condemned her to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Susanna cried out with a loud voice, and said: O eternal God, who knowest hidden things, who knowest all things before they come to pass,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou knowest that they have borne false witness against me: and behold I must die, whereas I have done none of these things, which these men have maliciously forged against me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord heard her voice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when she was led to be put to death, the Lord raised up the holy spirit of a young boy, whose name was Daniel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he cried out with a loud voice: I am clear from the blood of this woman.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then all the people turning themselves towards him, said: What meaneth this word that thou hast spoken?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he standing in the midst of them, said: Are ye so foolish, ye children of Israel, that without examination or knowledge of the truth, you have condemned a daughter of Israel?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Return to judgment, for they have borne false witness against her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So all the people turned again in haste, and the old men said to him: Come, and sit thou down among us, and shew it us: seeing God hath given thee the honour of old age.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel said to the people: Separate these two far from one another, and I will examine them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So when they were put asunder one from the other, he called one of them, and said to him: O thou that art grown old in evil days, now are thy sins come out, which thou hast committed before:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In judging unjust judgments, oppressing the innocent, and letting the guilty to go free, whereas the Lord saith: The innocent and the just thou shalt not kill.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now then if thou sawest her, tell me under what tree thou sawest them conversing together: He said: Under a mastic tree.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel said: Well hast thou lied against thy own head: for behold the angel of God having received the sentence of him, shall cut thee in two.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having put him aside, he commanded that the other should come, and he said to him: O thou seed of Chanaan, and not of Juda, beauty hath deceived thee, and lust hath perverted thy heart:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus did you do to the daughters of Israel, and they for fear conversed with you: but a daughter of Juda would not abide your wickedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now, therefore, tell me, under what tree didst thou take them conversing together. And he answered: Under a holm tree.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel said to him: Well hast thou also lied against thy own head: for the angel of the Lord waiteth with a sword to cut thee in two, and to destroy you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With that all the assembly cried out with a loud voice, and they blessed God, who saveth them that trust in him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they rose up against the two elders, (for Daniel had convicted them of false witness by their own mouth) and they did to them as they had maliciously dealt against their neighbour,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To fulfil the law of Moses: and they put them to death, and innocent blood was saved in that day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Helcias, and his wife, praised God, for their daughter, Susanna, with Joakim, her husband, and all her kindred, because there was no dishonesty found in her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel became great in the sight of the people from that day, and thence forward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And king Astyages was gathered to his fathers; and Cyrus, the Persian, received his kingdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel was the king’s guest, and was honoured above all his friends.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now the Babylonians had an idol called Bel: and there was spent upon him every day twelve great measures of fine flour, and forty sheep, and six vessels of wine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The king also worshipped him, and went every day to adore him: but Daniel adored his God. And the king said to him: Why dost thou not adore Bel?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he answered, and said to him: Because I do not worship idols made with hands, but the living God, that created heaven and earth, and hath power over all flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king said to him: Doth not Bel seem to thee to be a living god? Seest thou not how much he eateth and drinketh every day?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Daniel smiled, and said: O king, be not deceived: for this is but clay within, and brass without, neither hath he eaten at any time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king being angry, called for his priests, and said to them: If you tell me not who it is that eateth up these expenses, you shall die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you can shew that Bel eateth these things, Daniel shall die, because he hath blasphemed against Bel. And Daniel said to the king: Be it done according to thy word.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now the priests of Bel were seventy, besides their wives, and little ones, and children. And the king went with Daniel into the temple of Bel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priests of Bel said: Behold, we go out: and do thou, O king, set on the meats, and make ready the wine, and shut the door fast, and seal it with thy own ring:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou comest in the morning, if thou findest not that Bel hath eaten up all, we will suffer death, or else Daniel, that hath lied against us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they little regarded it, because they had made under the table a secret entrance, and they always came in by it, and consumed those things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So it came to pass after they were gone out, the king set the meats before Bel: and Daniel commanded his servants, and they brought ashes, and he sifted them all over the temple before the king: and going forth, they shut the door, and having sealed it with the king’s ring, they departed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the priests went in by night, according to their custom, with their wives, and their children: and they ate and drank up all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king arose early in the morning, and Daniel with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king said: Are the seals whole, Daniel? And he answered: They are whole, O king.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as soon as he had opened the door, the king looked upon the table, and cried out with a loud voice: Great art thou, O Bel, and there is not any deceit with thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel laughed: and he held the king, that he should not go in: and he said: Behold the pavement, mark whose footsteps these are.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king said: I see the footsteps of men, and women, and children. And the king was angry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he took the priests, and their wives, and their children: and they shewed him the private doors by which they came in, and consumed the things that were on the table.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The king, therefore, put them to death, and delivered Bel into the power of Daniel: who destroyed him and his temple.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there was a great dragon in that place, and the Babylonians worshipped him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king said to Daniel: Behold, thou canst not say now, that this is not a living god: adore him, therefore.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel said: I adore the Lord, my God: for he is the living God: but that is no living god.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But give me leave, O king, and I will kill this dragon without sword or club. And the king said, I give thee leave.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Daniel took pitch, and fat, and hair, and boiled them together: and he made lumps, and put them into the dragon’s mouth, and the dragon burst asunder. And he said: Behold him whom you worship.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Babylonians had heard this, they took great indignation: and being gathered together against the king, they said: The king is become a Jew. He hath destroyed Bel, he hath killed the dragon, and he hath put the priests to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they came to the king, and said: Deliver us Daniel, or else we will destroy thee and thy house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king saw that they pressed upon him violently: and being constrained by necessity: he delivered Daniel to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they cast him into the den of lions, and he was there six days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the den there were seven lions, and they had given to them two carcasses every day, and two sheep: but then they were not given unto them, that they might devour Daniel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now there was in Judea a prophet called Habacuc, and he had boiled pottage, and had broken bread in a bowl: and was going into the field, to carry it to the reapers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the angel of the Lord said to Habacuc: Carry the dinner which thou hast into Babylon, to Daniel, who is in the lions’ den.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Habacuc said: Lord, I never saw Babylon, nor do I know the den.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the angel of the Lord took him by the top of his head, and carried him by the hair of his head, and set him in Babylon, over the den, in the force of his spirit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Habacuc cried, saying: O Daniel, thou servant of God, take the dinner that God hath sent thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel said, Thou hast remembered me, O God, and thou hast not forsaken them that love thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Daniel arose, and ate. And the angel of the Lord presently set Habacuc again in his own place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And upon the seventh day the king came to bewail Daniel: and he came to the den, and looked in, and behold Daniel was sitting in the midst of the lions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king cried out with a loud voice, saying: Great art thou, O Lord, the God of Daniel. And he drew him out of the lions’ den.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But those that had been the cause of his destruction, he cast into the den, and they were devoured in a moment before him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the king said: Let all the inhabitants of the whole earth fear the God of Daniel: for he is the Saviour, working signs, and wonders in the earth: who hath delivered Daniel out of the lions’ den.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
